text: This research investigates the challenges prevailing in the higher education sector of Ethiopia in qualitative terms. A total of 89 articles, government policy documents, journals and PhD researches are analyzed, in addition to a set of interviews and focus group discussions that were conducted with different stakeholders in higher education institutions of Ethiopia. After thoroughly assessing the quality assurance initiatives established to develop higher education institutions and ensure high quality learning and teaching processes for students, it was found out that the establishments did not lead to any significant improvement in the overall performance of higher education sector in Ethiopia. Lack of adequate number of professionals in higher education institutions, improper utilization of information and communication technology, insufficient development of library and laboratory services and students’ inadequate living standards due to over crowded hostels are the key structural challenges in the higher education sector of Ethiopia. Moreover, it was also revealed that the majority of higher education institutions in Ethiopia provide low quality services below standard values which negatively affect their academic goals. Recommendations for measures to mitigate structural obstacles, infrastructure and human resource weaknesses are provided in this study.
